name: Google Reader API
cause: Google believed users were declining, despite massive protests from loyal users
lastWords: We know Reader has a devoted following who will be very sad to see it go.
epitaph: RSS never dies, but I'll go first
dependents: Feedly, Reeder, countless RSS clients forced to migrate

name: Google+ API
cause: User data breach + almost nobody used it
lastWords: We are shutting down Google+ for consumer accounts.
epitaph: I tried. I really tried.
dependents: Google+ login integrations, social share buttons

name: Parse API
cause: Facebook acquired it, decided it wasn't profitable, shut it down
lastWords: We're winding down the Parse service.
epitaph: BaaS pioneer, killed by corporate indifference
dependents: Tens of thousands of mobile app backends

name: Vine API
cause: Twitter couldn't figure out monetization, just killed it
lastWords: Nothing is going away just yet.
epitaph: 6 seconds changed the world, then the world forgot me
dependents: Countless short video creators, third-party clients

name: Google Hangouts API
cause: Replaced by Google Chat and Google Meet
lastWords: Hangouts users will be upgraded to Google Chat.
epitaph: Google's Nth chat app. Won't be the last.
dependents: Enterprise integrations, chatbots

name: Flickr 免费存储 API
cause: SmugMug acquired it, cut free 1TB storage to 1,000 photos
lastWords: Free members will be limited to 1,000 photos.
epitaph: Once the king of photos, lost to the times
dependents: Photography communities, third-party album apps

name: Yahoo Pipes
cause: Yahoo mass layoffs, Pipes deemed non-core
lastWords: We will be shutting down Pipes on September 30, 2015.
epitaph: Visual programming pioneer, born too early
dependents: Data aggregation workflows, RSS mashup apps

name: goo.gl URL Shortener API
cause: Replaced by Firebase Dynamic Links (which also died later)
lastWords: Starting March 30, 2019, we will be turning down support.
epitaph: Short link, short life
dependents: Countless apps' URL shortening features

name: Firebase Dynamic Links
cause: Google decided maintenance cost was too high
lastWords: Firebase Dynamic Links is deprecated and should be migrated away from.
epitaph: Killed goo.gl, then died itself. Ironic.
dependents: Mobile deep linking, marketing tracking

name: Heroku Free Tier
cause: Salesforce wanted to make money. Free tier gone.
lastWords: Starting November 28, 2022, free Heroku Dynos will no longer be available.
epitaph: A generation's first deploy platform, killed by monetization
dependents: Countless student projects, personal projects, small startups

name: Twitter API (免费版)
cause: Elon Musk acquired Twitter, made free API nearly unusable
lastWords: Starting February 9, we will no longer support free access to the Twitter API.
epitaph: The gold standard of open ecosystems, killed by new owner's greed
dependents: Tweetbot, thousands of bots, academic research, data analysis tools

name: Stadia API / 平台
cause: Gamers didn't buy in, Google lost patience
lastWords: We will be winding down Stadia over the coming months.
epitaph: The future of cloud gaming - just not my future
dependents: Game developers, Stadia exclusive games

name: Yahoo Weather API (免费版)
cause: Yahoo shut down free weather API access
lastWords: The API is no longer available for free use.
epitaph: Weather forecasts aren't free anymore, but weather still is
dependents: Weather apps, smart home integrations

name: Inbox by Gmail API
cause: Features merged into Gmail, Inbox shut down
lastWords: We're saying goodbye to Inbox by Gmail.
epitaph: Better than Gmail, so it had to die
dependents: Inbox users, email automation tools

name: Visual Studio Codespaces
cause: Consolidated into GitHub Codespaces
lastWords: Visual Studio Codespaces is being consolidated into GitHub Codespaces.
epitaph: Name stolen, soul stolen
dependents: VS Codespaces users

name: Google Allo
cause: Nobody used it. Google made another chat app and abandoned it again.
lastWords: We're pausing investment in Allo.
epitaph: Google chat app graveyard +1
dependents: Almost none, because almost nobody used it

name: Fabric / Crashlytics (独立版)
cause: Google acquired it and merged into Firebase
lastWords: Fabric is being sunset. Please migrate to Firebase.
epitaph: Being acquired is just another way to die
dependents: Hundreds of thousands of mobile apps' crash monitoring

name: mLab (免费 MongoDB 托管)
cause: Acquired by MongoDB Atlas and merged
lastWords: All mLab accounts have been migrated to MongoDB Atlas.
epitaph: Synonymous with free MongoDB, died by acquisition
dependents: Countless student projects and prototype apps

name: Google Cloud Print
cause: Chrome OS got native printing support, Cloud Print became useless
lastWords: Google Cloud Print will no longer be supported as of December 31, 2020.
epitaph: Cloud printing sounds cool, but nobody cared
dependents: Chromebook users, enterprise printing solutions

name: Internet Explorer
cause: Replaced by Edge, Microsoft finally let go
lastWords: Internet Explorer 11 has retired and is officially out of support.
epitaph: Not the best browser, but the best tool for downloading other browsers
dependents: Countless enterprise intranet systems, ActiveX controls

name: Bintray / JCenter
cause: JFrog decided to shut down free services
lastWords: The Bintray, JCenter, GoCenter, and ChartCenter services will be sunset.
epitaph: Half the Android ecosystem depended on me, then I vanished
dependents: Nearly all Android projects, Java libraries

name: Google Domains
cause: Google sold its domain business to Squarespace
lastWords: We've entered into an agreement with Squarespace.
epitaph: Can't even keep a domain business running. Impressive.
dependents: 10M+ domain holders

name: iTunes XML Library API
cause: macOS Catalina replaced iTunes with Music.app
lastWords: iTunes is being replaced by dedicated apps.
epitaph: End of an era. 1,000 songs in your pocket, now in the cloud.
dependents: DJ software, music management tools, playlist sync

name: OpenAI Codex API (独立版)
cause: Merged into GPT-3.5/GPT-4, no longer offered separately
lastWords: Codex models are now deprecated.
epitaph: The dawn of AI coding, replaced by its own descendants
dependents: Early GitHub Copilot, code generation tools

name: Adobe Flash / ActionScript
cause: Too many security vulnerabilities, HTML5 replaced it, browsers united to block it
lastWords: Adobe will no longer be supporting Flash Player after December 31, 2020.
epitaph: The entire internet's childhood, killed by progress
dependents: Countless Flash games, animation sites, video players

name: Picasa Web Albums API
cause: Replaced by Google Photos
lastWords: We're retiring Picasa to focus on Google Photos.
epitaph: Once the best photo manager, killed by its own sibling
dependents: Picasa desktop users, third-party album integrations

name: Windows Phone API / 平台
cause: Market share too low, developers wouldn't build for it, vicious cycle
lastWords: Windows 10 Mobile has reached end of support.
epitaph: Live Tiles were beautiful, but nobody was buying
dependents: Windows Phone app developers, Nokia Lumia users

name: Google Translate API (免费版)
cause: Too much abuse, Google switched to paid API
lastWords: The Google Translate API has been officially deprecated.
epitaph: The golden age of free translation, gone forever
dependents: Countless translation plugins, multilingual websites

name: HipChat API
cause: Atlassian acquired it, failed to compete with Slack, pivoted to Stride which also failed
lastWords: We will be ending HipChat and Stride services.
epitaph: Acquired twice, died twice
dependents: Enterprise chat integrations, DevOps notification bots

name: Yahoo Directory
cause: Search engines made manual directory categorization pointless
lastWords: Yahoo Directory will be retired at the end of 2014.
epitaph: The internet's first Yellow Pages, killed by search engines
dependents: Early internet users, SEO practitioners

name: Delicious API
cause: Changed hands multiple times, finally shut down
lastWords: Delicious will be shutting down.
epitaph: The OG of social bookmarking, died with no one to take over
dependents: Bookmark management tools, social sharing apps

name: Cortana Skills Kit / API
cause: Microsoft abandoned consumer Cortana, pivoted to enterprise
lastWords: Cortana in Windows is being deprecated.
epitaph: Hey Cortana... Goodbye Cortana
dependents: Cortana skill developers, smart home integrations

name: Amazon Drive API
cause: Amazon closed third-party API access, then killed the whole service
lastWords: Amazon Drive is being discontinued.
epitaph: Even Amazon didn't want to run a cloud drive
dependents: Third-party backup tools, file sync apps

name: Bitbucket Mercurial 支持
cause: Git won the version control war, too few Mercurial users
lastWords: We will be sunsetting Mercurial support on Bitbucket.
epitaph: Git vs Mercurial - winner takes all
dependents: Mercurial projects, Python community (CPython used Hg)

name: Windows Desktop Gadgets API
cause: Severe security vulnerabilities, Microsoft killed the entire feature
lastWords: Gadgets have been discontinued due to serious vulnerabilities.
epitaph: Desktop widget pioneer, killed by security flaws
dependents: Windows Vista/7 desktop gadget developers

name: Foursquare API v2 (免费版)
cause: Foursquare pivoted to B2B, free API heavily restricted then closed
lastWords: We're making changes to our developer offerings.
epitaph: Creator of check-in culture, killed by its own pivot
dependents: LBS apps, check-in social products

name: IFTTT 免费无限 Applets
cause: Free users went from unlimited Applets to only 3
lastWords: We're introducing IFTTT Pro.
epitaph: Democratized automation, killed by a paywall
dependents: Smart home users, automation workflows

name: Google Fit REST API
cause: Google replaced it with Health Connect, REST API deprecated
lastWords: Google Fit APIs are deprecated. Migrate to Health Connect.
epitaph: The dream of open health data, shattered again
dependents: Fitness apps, health data analysis tools

name: Ubuntu Touch / Unity API
cause: Canonical abandoned phone/tablet plans, returned to server market
lastWords: We will shift our default Ubuntu desktop back to GNOME.
epitaph: The Linux phone dream - woke up
dependents: Ubuntu Phone developers, Unity desktop users

name: Tumblr API v1
cause: Replaced by v2 and deprecated, Tumblr itself in decline
lastWords: API v1 is no longer supported.
epitaph: The rebellious teen of blog platforms, lost itself growing up
dependents: Tumblr third-party clients, blog migration tools
